member.name: T:FiveOS.Plugins.IFiveOSHost
member.summary: Services the host exposes to plugins via . The interface is intentionally small — every method is a stable affordance plugins can rely on across host versions. New capabilities should be added as new methods/properties (never break existing ones).

member.name: P:FiveOS.Plugins.IFiveOSHost.HostVersion
member.summary: Semver string of the running host (e.g. "0.2.3"). Plugins can compare against their declared minHostVersion if they want to gracefully degrade on older hosts.

member.name: P:FiveOS.Plugins.IFiveOSHost.PluginDirectory
member.summary: Absolute path to this plugin's own folder under %AppData%\FiveOS\plugins\<name>\. Use it to resolve bundled assets (PNGs, JSON tables, etc.) without hard-coding paths.

member.name: M:FiveOS.Plugins.IFiveOSHost.GetSetting(System.String)
member.summary: Read a plugin-scoped string setting. Keys are namespaced by the plugin id internally so two plugins can use the same key name without collision. Returns null if the key was never set.

member.name: M:FiveOS.Plugins.IFiveOSHost.SetSetting(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Persist a plugin-scoped string setting. Pass null to clear.

member.name: M:FiveOS.Plugins.IFiveOSHost.Toast(System.String)
member.summary: Show a non-blocking toast / status update to the user. The host decides where it surfaces (status bar, transient overlay, etc.) — plugins should treat this as fire-and-forget.

member.name: T:FiveOS.Plugins.IFiveOSPlugin
member.summary:
The contract a .NET FiveOS plugin must implement. Plugin DLLs reference FiveOS.Plugins.Sdk.dll (a tiny stable assembly) and expose a single public type implementing this interface — PluginManager reflects over plugin.json:entry and instantiates the first match.
Lifecycle: 1. The host instantiates the plugin via its parameterless constructor. 2. The host calls exactly once with a host reference. Plugins should stash this and use it for settings, toasts, and folder lookups. 3. The host calls when the plugin's rail entry is first activated. The returned is cached for the session.

member.name: P:FiveOS.Plugins.IFiveOSPlugin.Id
member.summary: Stable identifier (lowercase, dash-separated). Persisted in UserSettings to remember enable state and per-plugin preferences.

member.name: P:FiveOS.Plugins.IFiveOSPlugin.Name
member.summary: Human-readable display name. Shown in the rail tooltip and in Settings → Addons.

member.name: P:FiveOS.Plugins.IFiveOSPlugin.Description
member.summary: One-line description shown under the name in the addons list.

member.name: M:FiveOS.Plugins.IFiveOSPlugin.Initialize(FiveOS.Plugins.IFiveOSHost)
member.summary: Called once before . Plugins should stash the host reference and use it later for settings, toasts, etc. Default implementation does nothing — override only if needed.

member.name: M:FiveOS.Plugins.IFiveOSPlugin.CreateView
member.summary: Build the WPF view that will be hosted in the main pane when this plugin is the active rail entry. Called once per session — the host caches the returned control.
